remaining_limits: official China sports lottery odds missing
final injury and minutes-limit confirmation incomplete

scale_definition.positive: favorable tactical adjustment for the named team
scale_definition.negative: unfavorable adjustment or higher tactical risk for the named team
scale_definition.cap_rule: Do not add magnitudes linearly. Cap overlap with player_state, attack/defense scores, round-1 goals/concessions, injuries and set-piece events.

inputs_read: 比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/K组/2026-06-17_葡萄牙_1-1_刚果金_复盘.md
比赛/已完成比赛/小组赛/K组/2026-06-17_乌兹别克斯坦_1-3_哥伦比亚_复盘.md
队伍/乌兹别克斯坦/成员表.md

matches.match_cn: 葡萄牙 vs 乌兹别克斯坦
matches.tactical_summary: Portugal's control, wide depth and set-piece quality should stress Uzbekistan's 3-4-3/5-4-1 block. Uzbekistan's credible route is to keep the match compact, then use Shomurodov as a target and Fayzullaev/Khamdamov as transition or second-phase creators. Houston heat/humidity and Portugal's Ronaldo/Dias role uncertainty keep the package source-limited.

evidence: Portugal can press from a 4-2-3-1/4-3-3 base.
Uzbekistan's first-round build-up was limited and improved only after halftime changes.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Small territory/regain boost only; Ronaldo role can cap first-line pressure.

double_counting_risk.note: Do not double-count Ronaldo/pressing-role adjustments.

evidence: Portugal use Vitinha, Bruno Fernandes, Bernardo Silva and Joao Neves for control.
Uzbekistan do not profile as a sustained high-press side.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Use to lower Portugal turnover risk; cap against baseline team-strength and control score.

double_counting_risk.note: Joao Neves and Pedro Neto round-1 form already raise state inputs.

evidence: Portugal have Leao, Neto and Conceicao as direct runners.
Uzbekistan wing-back spaces and post-turnover recovery were stressed by Colombia.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Moderate boost; stronger only if Portugal start more pace and fewer static attackers.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlaps with Neto assist and attacking-depth score.

evidence: Portugal tactics packet flags fullback-space and second-ball defense risk.
Uzbekistan keep Shomurodov and Fayzullaev as direct outlets.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Preserve Uzbekistan counter tail when Portugal fullbacks push high.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlap with Fayzullaev/Shomurodov player-state.

evidence: Portugal's wide options include Neto, Leao, Conceicao, Cancelo and Nuno Mendes.
Uzbekistan tactics packet identifies wing-back-behind risk.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Main Portugal matchup edge; cap if lineup is slower or Uzbekistan stay in a deep back five.

double_counting_risk.note: Wide events from round 1 are already in Portugal player_state.

evidence: Portugal have Vitinha/Bruno/Joao Neves control profiles.
Uzbekistan's midfield was more reactive against Colombia.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Use as possession stability, not an automatic chance-quality add.

double_counting_risk.note: Joao Neves goal and midfield form can be counted twice.

evidence: Portugal tactics packet marks set pieces high with Ronaldo/Dias/Bruno profiles.
Uzbekistan have Khusanov and back-three height but conceded pressure from wide/dead-ball phases.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Keep as capped secondary edge because first-round Portugal heading/set-piece evidence already exists.

double_counting_risk.note: Joao Neves headed goal and Portugal set-piece strength overlap.

evidence: Portugal conceded from a set-piece second phase to DR Congo.
Uzbekistan have Shomurodov and Fayzullaev for direct/second-ball attacks.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Small clean-sheet-tail risk; do not turn it into a broad defense downgrade.

double_counting_risk.note: May overlap with Portugal round-1 concession adjustment.

evidence: Portugal used Conceicao, Leao, Semedo and Ramos but did not turn first-round control into a win.
Martinez has deep bench but must balance Ronaldo minutes and pressure intensity.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Bench depth is positive; game-management risk caps the number.

double_counting_risk.note: Bench attackers' state and attack depth may already be encoded.

evidence: Portugal led early in round 1 but did not sustain separation.
Uzbekistan improved after halftime changes when chasing.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Condition on first goal and halftime state; do not use as unconditional boost.

double_counting_risk.note: Can double-count round-1 narrative and Portugal favorite strength.

evidence: Uzbekistan tactics packet says long high press is unlikely.
First round used 3-4-3 dropping to 5-4-1.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Do not assign high-regain value unless official XI changes shape.

double_counting_risk.note: Mostly structural.

evidence: Uzbekistan first-round progression was limited before halftime changes.
Portugal can crowd first passes through midfield and wide traps.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Increase long-ball/clearance assumptions; soften if Khamdamov/Sayfiev start.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlap with Yusupov/Shomurodov/Fayzullaev state only if modeled separately.

evidence: Fayzullaev scored and was more active after halftime.
Shomurodov created the first-round goal action but also had a turnover before Colombia's second.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Preserve underdog counter route; cap because it is already in player_state.

double_counting_risk.note: High overlap with Fayzullaev/Shomurodov form_status.

evidence: Uzbekistan conceded after a midfield turnover and late wide-cross sequence vs Colombia.
Portugal have multiple wide runners and second-wave midfielders.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Apply when Uzbekistan chase or wing-backs advance.

double_counting_risk.note: Can double-count Portugal wide/transition factors.

evidence: Uzbekistan tactics packet flags wing-back-behind risk.
Portugal wide depth is one of the clearest matchup stresses.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Net defensive weakness; keep some attacking wing-back value if Khamdamov/Sayfiev start.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlap with Portugal wide_channel_advantage.

evidence: Portugal midfield profiles are stronger in control.
Uzbekistan central midfield had limited first-round control.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Lower settled possession value but keep low-block resistance separate.

double_counting_risk.note: May overlap with baseline strength gap.

evidence: Uzbekistan have Khusanov and back-three aerial value.
Portugal also carry stronger high-point and delivery profiles.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Small tail only; set-piece map incomplete.

double_counting_risk.note: Could overlap with Khusanov defensive/offensive state.

evidence: Back five protects the box but can be pulled wide.
Colombia punished Uzbekistan in transition and late wide delivery.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Risk rises if trailing; lower if compact 5-4-1 holds early.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlap with conceded-goals and defender form states.

evidence: Cannavaro halftime changes improved progression and created the first-round goal spell.
Initial setup was passive and official second-round XI is unknown.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Small positive for adjustment capacity, but do not boost starting baseline.

double_counting_risk.note: Khamdamov/Sayfiev substitute states can be double-counted.

evidence: Uzbekistan were competitive after equalizing but conceded again quickly.
Low-block path improves if level early; chasing exposes wing-backs.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Bimodal: reduce risk in low-event level state, increase sharply when chasing.

double_counting_risk.note: Can double-count transition_defense_risk and round-1 concession narrative.

matches.match_cn: 哥伦比亚 vs 刚果金
matches.tactical_summary: Colombia's strongest provisional edges are Luis Diaz-led wide/transition attacks, James service, set pieces and late bench impact. DR Congo's credible route is compact 5-3-2 defending, Wissa/Bakambu direct transition and set-piece second balls; travel to Guadalajara and altitude/weather gaps keep confidence capped.

evidence: Colombia tactics packet supports mid/high press on wide and first-pass lanes.
DR Congo prefer direct release under pressure.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Use for territory and forced direct play; not a pure shot boost.

double_counting_risk.note: Mostly structural.

evidence: Colombia have James, Lerma and center-back speed/protection.
DR Congo pressing profile is not sustained high.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Small possession-security boost; cap for physical duel pressure.

double_counting_risk.note: James/Lerma state may overlap.

evidence: Luis Diaz scored/assisted and is Colombia's primary left-channel accelerator.
Campaz and Cucho produced a late substitute goal chain.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Largest Colombia edge; cap because player_state already strongly upgrades Diaz/Campaz/Cucho.

double_counting_risk.note: Very high overlap with first-round goals and attacking player form.

evidence: DR Congo have Wissa/Bakambu/Sadiki direct routes.
Colombia fullbacks can push high, creating counter space.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Preserve DR Congo counter tail; lower if Colombia keep fullback restraint.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlap with Wissa/Bakambu player_state.

evidence: Luis Diaz, Munoz and Arias provide strong wide/right-left pressure.
DR Congo wing-backs defended well but can be pinned and forced deep.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Key Colombia matchup edge; cap with transition_attack_adjustment.

double_counting_risk.note: Diaz/Munoz first-round goal chain already counted.

evidence: James sets tempo and set pieces; Lerma/Puerta give duel/control base.
DR Congo are more reactive in settled phases.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Use as field tilt and chance-supply support, not duplicate wide attack.

double_counting_risk.note: James/Puerta/Lerma states can overlap with attack score.

evidence: Colombia tactics packet marks James delivery and aerial targets as high-value.
DR Congo also have set-piece strength, reducing net edge.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Moderate edge only; avoid double-counting James creation and Colombia goals.

double_counting_risk.note: Set-piece service overlaps with James and team attack grade.

evidence: Colombia fullbacks and wide players can leave counter lanes.
DR Congo transition route through Wissa/Bakambu is credible.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlap with DR Congo transition_attack_adjustment.

evidence: Campaz scored after coming on; Cucho assisted late vs Uzbekistan.
Lorenzo kept a counter outlet while rotating late.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: Late-bench positive, but cap because substitute output is already in player_state.

double_counting_risk.note: Campaz/Cucho first-round events already reflected.

evidence: Colombia scored after Uzbekistan equalized and again late through substitutes.
Lorenzo profile keeps counter outlet when leading.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Use as resilience/late-threat factor, capped against transition and substitution boosts.

double_counting_risk.note: May double-count first-round late goal and substitute impact.

evidence: DR Congo mainly defended compactly vs Portugal.
They can still make phase pressure and physical contests matter.
pressing_adjustment.model_note: Tiny disruption value only; do not model sustained high press.

double_counting_risk.note: Mostly structural.

evidence: DR Congo tactics packet discourages risky short build-up against stronger pressure.
Colombia can lock wide/DM first passes.
build_up_resistance_adjustment.model_note: Increase direct-play share and second-ball variance.

double_counting_risk.note: Not primarily player-state driven.

evidence: Wissa scored and remained active in counters.
Bakambu hit the post and Sadiki added direct progression after coming on.
transition_attack_adjustment.model_note: Primary DR Congo upside; cap because Wissa/Bakambu/Sadiki player states already capture it.

double_counting_risk.note: High overlap with round-1 player_state and counter-path notes.

evidence: Colombia's first-round goals included wide/transition and substitute late impact.
DR Congo wing-backs can be pinned or stretched.
transition_defense_risk.model_note: Main downside if DR Congo chase or wing-backs step high.

double_counting_risk.note: Overlaps with Colombia transition and wide-channel factors.

evidence: Wan-Bissaka and Masuaku give defensive wing-back value.
Colombia's Diaz/Munoz/Arias profile is stronger in the same lanes.
wide_channel_advantage.model_note: Do not give DR Congo a net wide edge; keep only counter-release value.

double_counting_risk.note: May overlap with wing-back defensive form.

evidence: Colombia have James/Lerma/Puerta/Rios control and duel options.
DR Congo were more comfortable without the ball vs Portugal.
central_control_advantage.model_note: Lower settled-possession assumptions, not counter threat.

double_counting_risk.note: Partly overlaps with Colombia strength and DR Congo attack ceiling.

evidence: DR Congo equalized through corner/second-phase header vs Portugal.
Mbemba, Bakambu and Wissa give aerial/box presence.
set_piece_advantage.model_note: Keep as important underdog tail but cap heavily due first-round goal already in player_state.

double_counting_risk.note: Wissa goal and set-piece path already reflected.

evidence: DR Congo defended well in a 5-3-2 but absorbed long pressure.
Colombia have quicker wide runners than Portugal's first-round rhythm showed.
defensive_line_risk.model_note: Risk is moderate, reduced if five-back stays compact.

double_counting_risk.note: Defender form from Portugal draw may already be credited.

evidence: Desabre added Sadiki and late defensive/forward changes vs Portugal.
Full substitution tendencies by state remain incomplete.
coach_substitution_risk.model_note: No net adjustment; wait for lineup and bench plan.

double_counting_risk.note: Sadiki late impact may already be encoded.

evidence: DR Congo proved they can stay compact and use set-piece/counter windows.
If trailing, their structure opens and Colombia transition risk rises.
game_state_tendency.model_note: Positive only in level/low-event state; negative if forced to chase.

double_counting_risk.note: Can double-count transition and set-piece factors if applied unconditionally.

data_gaps.impact: Portugal Ronaldo/Dias plan, Uzbekistan back-five personnel, Colombia James/Diaz load and DR Congo 5-3-2 personnel cannot be locked.

data_gaps.gap: official China sports lottery odds missing
data_gaps.impact: No betting or market execution language is allowed.
Model cannot do final market calibration from this tactical packet alone.

data_gaps.gap: fresh injury and minutes-limit confirmation incomplete
data_gaps.impact: Ruben Dias conflict, Ronaldo load, Uzbekistan partial player_state, DR Congo travel recovery and Colombia creator minutes remain uncertain.
Portugal, DR Congo and Colombia set-piece factors need caps because round-1 goals/events already inform player_state.

data_gaps.gap: roster JSON schemas nested for Uzbekistan/Colombia
data_gaps.impact: Facts are available through player_state and member tables, but automated roster parsers should recurse into nested player arrays.

model_agent: Fixtures are confirmed by data refresh, but packet remains partial_source_limited because lineups, official odds and final injury/minute updates are missing.

Portugal vs Uzbekistan: strongest tactical modifiers are Portugal wide_channel_advantage, Portugal build_up_resistance, Uzbekistan transition_defense_risk and Uzbekistan counter tail through Fayzullaev/Shomurodov.
Colombia vs DR Congo: strongest modifiers are Colombia transition_attack, Colombia wide_channel_advantage, DR Congo set_piece_advantage and DR Congo transition_attack.
Cap all set-piece factors because first-round goals already entered player_state and postmatch narratives.
Do not linearly add Colombia transition_attack, wide_channel_advantage and coach_substitution_risk; they share Diaz/Campaz/Cucho evidence.
red_team: Challenge Portugal favorite/brand and Ronaldo role double counting.
Challenge whether Uzbekistan's second-half improvement is being over-weighted from a short sample.
Challenge DR Congo defensive resilience after the Portugal draw: compactness was real but may not transfer to Colombia's wide-speed profile.
Challenge Colombia first-round goals, player_state and tactical factors for triple-counting Luis Diaz/Campaz/Cucho.
Challenge environment adjustments: Houston heat and Guadalajara altitude are partial context, not lineup-quality facts.

notes: Filled after data refresh confirmed fixtures.
No final score, win/draw/loss probability, or betting recommendation is provided.
